description: A falling stone, a swinging tool, or a moving hand may 'patore' something in one brief forceful impact. The verb reports the contact before deciding whether it was deliberate or harmful. 'pesa' (push) sustains force in a direction, while 'pukeri' (damage) gives the worsened result.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into open central 'a'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't', then round the lips into stressed middle-height back 'o'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge and finish with middle-height front unrounded 'e'.
usage_notes: The striking body or object is the subject and what receives the impact is the object. Aspect and an instrument phrase supply the mechanics; another clause can state intent or outcome.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A neutral event report can keep cause, intent, harm, and responsibility open until the speaker has evidence for each one.

semantic_domains.activity: one body or object making a brief forceful impact on another
semantic_domains.physical: the contact event produced by a strike or collision
